segments.text: So when the next Sutta is 5.20.196 the Buddha said monks to the Tathagatha
arahan samasambuddha before his full awakening when he was not yet wholly
awakened but a being awakening there came five great dreams what five monks
the Tathagatha arahan samasambuddha before his full awakening when he was
not yet wholly awakened and but a being awakening dream that this mighty world
was his bed of state the mountain king Himalaya his pillow that his left hand
rested on the eastern sea his right on the western sea and that both his feet
rested on the southern sea monks to the Tathagatha arahan samasambuddha before
his full awakening when he was not yet wholly awakened and but a being awakening
this first great dream came again he dreamed that they went out from his
naval Tyria grass and it stopped not until it touched the clouds monks to the
Tathagatha before his full awakening and this second dream came again he dream
that white worms with black heads crept up over his feet as far as his knees
and covered them monks to him this third dream came again he dreamed that there
came four birds of varied colors from the four quarters of the world and they
fell at his feet and became all white monks to him this fourth dream came again
he dreamed that he walked to and fro on a great mountain of dung and was
unbismirred with dung monks to him this fifth dream came monks when indeed to
the Tathagatha arahan samasambuddha before his full awakening when he was not
yet fully awakened and but a being awakening there came the dream that
this great world was his bed of state monks by the Tathagatha arahan fully
awake to the highest and unsurpassed full awakening to the highest was wholly
awakened within him to him wholly awakening this first dream came when to
him there came the dream that grass went out from his navel monks by the
Tathagatha the Aryan eightfold way was wholly awakened within him and well
proclaimed as far as devas and men exist to him wholly awakening this second
dream came when to him there came the dream that white worms with black heads
crept up to his knees months many white robe householders found life long
refuge in the Tathagatha to him wholly awakening this third dream came when to
him there came the dream that the four birds of the four quarters fell white
at his feet these four cars noble Brahmin merchants and laborers went forth
from the home to the homeless life into that dhamma Vinaya taught by the
Tathagatha and realized unsurpassed liberation to him wholly awakening this
fourth dream came when he dreamed that he walked on the mountain of dung monks
the Tathagatha received the requisites robes arms lodging and medicines and in
them the Tathagatha found enjoyment yet was not tied trust nor attached there
to seeing the danger wise as to the escape there from to him wholly
awakening this fifth dream came months to the Tathagatha Arahan Samasambuddha
before his full awakening when he know when he was not yet fully awakened and
was but a being awakening there came these five great dreams as the end of
the sutta this sutta is saying that the before the Buddha was enlightened these
five dreams came to tell him that what was about to happen dreams are quite
interesting sometimes it explains our problem in quite clearly sometimes we
have some problem and we dream if we analyze the dream it is trying to tell
us something so in this case or so sometimes now what is going to happen
also it can tell us so in this case the Buddha had five interesting dreams the
first one was he slept over the whole world the whole world was like was like
his bed and what this dream was trying to tell him was that he was going to be
like the king of the world the highest in the world that means he was going to
become enlightened that was the meaning of the first dream the second one from
his navel from his navel from his tummy there the grass came out and it spread
until it touched the clouds in the sky and this man that he was going to
proclaim the Aryan Eightfold Way out of samsara up to the heavens and devas and
men would come to know of this the third one he dream that white worms with
black heads crept up his knees and covered his his legs up to his knees
this man that lay people many many lay people would take refuge in him with
him and become his disciples the fourth one that the four birds of different
colors fell at his feet and became white these men that the four casts would
go forth under him and become monks and attain liberation and the last one that
he walked on a mountain of excrement or down man that he he looked at these
requisites as similar to down but it could be enjoyed but if you become
attached to it then it becomes like excrement and because it will dirty you
but he was able to walk on them without the thing his body which means that he
was not attached to them this quite interesting because monks not only
monks lay people also these things in the world that come to us and we make use
of them if you are wise we know how to enjoy them without getting dirty sometimes
you can you can see some people and they inherit property and then they change
for the worse and or they work very hard and they become very rich and then they
become so attached to their property and wealth that this this wealth dirties
them just like makes them unclean they have become defiled by these wealth and
property so in that case the wealth and property is like excrement and down so
that is a warning that we should treat these things in the world very carefully
if we know how to use them wisely and we are not dirty we are not defiled by
them but if we become too attached to them then they drag us down they defile
us there's one Sutta Pratapala Sutta in the Madhima Nikaya where son of a very
rich family he went for and after becoming an arahan he came back to his
house and his parents were still trying to persuade him to come back to the lay
life to take back all his property and he told his his parents he said the best
thing to do with all this money and all this property is to throw in the river
throw in a way because otherwise he's gonna drag you down the attachment to
this property is going to drag you down to a bad rebirth so that also shows how
we should be careful of all these wealth and property in the world the next
Sutta is five point twenty point one nine eight the Buddha said monks if a word
have five marks it is well spoken not ill-spoken nor is it blame worthy nor
blameable by the wise it is spoken in season or timely it is spoken in truth
it is spoken softly it is spoken about the beneficial it is spoken with the
heart of goodwill fairly monks if a word have these five marks it is well
spoken not ill-spoken nor is it blame worthy nor blameable by the wise as the
end of the Sutta here the Buddha is saying that good speech have five
qualities the first one it is timely spoken very timely sometimes we have
something to say sometimes to advise some body but we must know the proper
time to tell that person if that person is very upset or very angry and we
choose that time to tell that person that person might not accept it so
speaking in the right time is important the second one it what you speak
should be truthful should be truthful the third one it should be spoken
gently not harshly spoken gently and then people can accept it better the
fourth one it should be beneficial and the fifth one is will be spoken with a
heart of goodwill with a good intention these are the marks of a good
speech the next Sutta is five point twenty point one nine nine monks when
virtuous world renunciants approach a family men there in five ways beget great
merit in what five monks when virtuous world renunciants approach men seeing
them make calm their hearts monks that leads heavenward and the family then
has stepped a step along the way or when they approach men stand up salute and
offer a seat monks that leads to birth in higher rank and the family then has
stepped a step along the way or they rid themselves of all stain of meanness
monks that leads to great power and the family then has stepped a step along the
way or they share their food as means and power allow monks that leads to great
wealth and the family then has stepped a step along the way or when virtuous
world renunciants approach men ask make question about and listen to dumb
monks that leads to great wisdom and the family then has stepped a step along
the way when virtuous world renunciants approach a family men there in these
five ways beget great merit that's the end of the Sutta the Sutta saying when
renunciant real renunciant approaches and people are respectful in the
different ways they great they get great merit I think earlier you remember one
of the suttas we read was that there are four fields of merit and also four
fields of demerit this one is a Buddha but the Buddha is no more with us the
second one is disciples of the Buddha meaning monks and nuns third one is
mother the fourth one is father so these four persons we should be very careful
in our conduct towards them so in this case like if we we have we have proper
conduct towards renunciants then we get great merit there's also another Sutta
where devas different groups of devas came to see the Buddha at at different
times and they told the Buddha that when monks approach some of them in like
they were very happy to see monks and then or the first one that perhaps they
were not happy to see monks and but because of other good deeds that they
had done they were reborn in the heavens but because they were not happy to see
monks they were deficient in certain qualities because devas some of them
they are brighter their body shines brighter than others some are darker
some are brighter depending on how much merit they have and and that that
brightness in a way denotes how much power they have and so like this deva
they come they said that because they were not happy to see monks so they
suffered as a result they were less powerful in the heavenly realm and then
another group of devas came to to see the Buddha and they said when they saw
monks they they were not not happy they they received the monks but they did
not they were not respectful so because they were not respectful towards the
monks also when they were born in heaven they were also deficient in certain
qualities and so they regretted and then and later another group of devas came
to see the Buddha and they said when they saw monks they were happy they were
respectful but they did not like share the food with the monks and because they
did not give food to the monks when they were reborn in the deva realm but they
also were deficient in certain qualities and they regretted that and another
group said that they did all these things but they did not ask about dhamma they
did not bother to learn about dhamma and also they regretted and then finally
some other group said that they did all these things they they were happy to see
the monks they were respectful towards the monks they offered food they listen
to dhamma etc and when they were born in the heavens they had great power had
great qualities and so they were very happy and so the the the Buddha
confirmed all that so you can see depending on our kamma we are reborn
according to our kamma now the sutra 5.21.201 once when the exalted one
was dwelling near kimbila in the bamboo grove the variable kimbila visited him
and after saluting sat down at one side so seated he spoke thus to the exalted
one Lord what is the cause what is the reason whereby when the exalted one has
passed away completely through dhamma saddhamma does not become long-lasting
the Buddha said suppose kimbila after the tathagata has passed away completely the
monks and nuns lay disciples both men and women live without reverence without
heed without respect for the teacher without respect without heed for dhamma
without respect without heed for sangha for the training without respect without
heed for one another this is the cause this is the reason whereby when the
tathagata has passed away completely true dhamma does not become long-lasting
and what Lord is the cause the reason whereby after the tathagata has passed
away true dhamma becomes long-lasting suppose kimbila monks and nuns lay men
and lay women disciples respect and give heed to the teacher the dhamma the
sangha the training we veer and give heed to one another this is the cause the
reason whereby when the tathagata has passed away true dhamma becomes long-lasting
that's the end of the sutta this is one of the sutras which tries to say why the
true dhamma the discourses of the Buddha and does not last long here is saying
that the first three and without respect for the the Buddha the dhamma and the
sangha without respect for the Buddha means probably that instead of falling
recognizing our Buddha people revere other Buddhas instead of acknowledging
our teacher is the Buddha we look to others other other beings to respect
us the teacher without reverence for the dhamma is that we don't take the dhamma
vinaya as our teacher in the sutras the Buddha said now we should take the
dhamma vinaya as our teacher we should always try to learn what is in the
dhamma and the vinaya and be heedful of the teachings there the third one is no
respect for the sangha there might be some reason for this perhaps some people
they the the sangha perhaps might not be practicing well might not keep the
precepts very well and people lose respect for the sangha and that would
cause a decline but we have to remember we lay people are supposed to support
the sangha to keep the satsana Buddha satsana going so the Buddha said if in
the future even if those who wear the yellow robes are corrupt still if you
support the sangha then the merit is still very great because you help to
perpetuate the sangha by helping to perpetuate the sangha even if at a
certain time the sangha is corrupt this possible later on that our hands will
still be around whereas if you don't support the sangha the sangha is
terminated cut off then and there is no possible for good monks to appear later
no reverence for the training is a training of the monks life keeping the
precepts etc and without heed for one another is we don't when we don't live
in harmony if we don't live in harmony that is one cause for the true dhamma to
pass away there are other reasons given in other sutras but this they just stop here for the moment
sutra number five point twenty one point two zero two the Buddha said
monks there are these five advantages from hearing dhamma one five
he hears things not heard purges things hurt dispels doubt make straight his view
and his heart becomes calm verily monks these are the five advantages from
hearing dhamma the end of the sutra the first one he hears things not heard and
means he learns the truth or the reality of existence purges things hurt that
means he gets rid of wrong views dispels doubt makes straight his view that means
he gets right view and his heart becomes calm because from the joy of listening to
dhamma joy joy arises and then the mind becomes tranquil so these are the five advantages from
hearing dhamma now in the samyuta nikaya 46.4.8 and the sutra the Buddha said that when one listens
to dhamma attentively the five hindrances exist not and the seven bojanga factors of enlightenment
are complete which means that when one listens to dhamma attentively one can become an aria
because the five hindrances not existing and the seven bojanga being complete
are conditions for attainment of aria hood and also in the madhima nikaya number 43
it is stated that there are two conditions for attaining right view listening to dhamma and
having proper attention the attainment of right view in the one of the sutras in angutra nikaya
means that a person understands the four arian truths and which means that when a person gets
person attains right view he also becomes an aria so this again confirms that listening to dhamma
one can become an aria and then in the angutra nikaya 5.25 it is said that if right view is
assisted by five things it leads to liberation or attainment of arahan hood moral conduct listening
to dhamma discussion of dhamma samatha and vipassana that means tranquilization and contemplation
so tranquilization and contemplation are the two aspects of meditation and on in addition to
meditation you must have moral conduct dhamma listening to the dhamma and discussion of dhamma
to attain liberation this again shows the importance of listening to dhamma and in another
sutra angutra nikaya 5.26 that it is stated there are five ways to attain aria hood listening to
dhamma teaching the dhamma repeating dhamma reflecting on dhamma and reflecting on the
object of concentration that means during meditation so that again shows that listening
to dhamma is very important and the next sutra is 5.21.207 the Buddha said monks there are these
five advantages from gruel or rise for richer what five the checks hunger keeps of thirst regulates
win cleanses the bladder and digest raw remnants of food barely monks these are the five advantages
of gruel or rise for rich in the monks vinaya the buddha taught his disciples his monks to take
one meal a day and it's one main meal but he did give certain allowances that monks could take in
the morning which did not constitute a meal things like taking milk taking this rice porridge
and other medicinal kinds of food that was condition considered medicinal of medicinal
value so because rice porridge had some advantages that's why the Buddha allowed
it to be taken in the morning for even for a one meal a day monk the next sutra is 5.21.209
monks there are these five disadvantages to one preaching dhamma in a long
long drawn plain song voice what five he's either carried away himself by the sound
or others are carried away thereby or householders are offended and say just as we sing for sure
these recluse sakyam sun sing or as he strives after purity of sound there is a break in concentration
and folk coming after fall into the way of wrong views or fall into the same views
now early monks these are the five disadvantages to one preaching dhamma in a long drawn plain song
voice so this sutra shows that in the Theravada tradition monks are not supposed to chant in a
in a long drawn plain song voice that means not in a like in a singing way
although this is done in Mahayana tradition it is not allowed in the Theravada tradition that's why
the Theravada chants are quite monotonous and it is for these because of this sutra like this
